name: Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel
region_intro: Mountain biking around Orce, located within the Granada Geopark in southeastern Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features dramatic contrasts, transitioning from lush pastoral valleys to arid, rugged badlands characterized by canyons and ravines. This geological landscape, shaped by an ancient lake, provides a complex network of trails through mountains and unique rock formations. Best mountain bike trails around Orce The most popular mountain bike trail is Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel, a 38.2 miles (61.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses varied terrain, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz, a difficult 46.7 miles (75.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and mountain views, characteristic of the Sierra de La Sagra area. Local mountain bikers also love the Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce, a 25.0 miles (40.3 km) trail leading through the unique geological formations of the Granada Geopark, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Orce is defined by rugged mountain scenery, arid badlands, and geological formations within the Granada Geopark. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Orce are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Orce's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on mountain bike trails around Orce?
A: Mountain biking around Orce offers a diverse and often challenging experience. The terrain transitions from lush pastoral valleys to arid, rugged badlands, characterized by canyons, ravines, and unique rock formations within the Granada Geopark. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, with many featuring significant elevation changes and loose, rocky, and dry trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209016067/orce-al-bosque-circular-por-el-cordel-de-puerto-de-chirivel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel&lt;/a&gt; route is considered difficult, covering over 61 km with more than 1200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes with significant elevation gain in the Orce area?
A: Yes, Orce is known for its rugged mountain scenery and offers several challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199529164/huescar-to-mirador-de-la-sagra-circular-via-cerro-perico-ruiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 75 km path with over 1400 meters of ascent, providing extensive mountain views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199756537/great-trail-of-the-first-settlers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Trail of the First Settlers&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 91 km with nearly 1460 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Orce?
A: The Orce area, situated within the Granada Geopark, is rich in unique natural and historical features. You can explore the distinctive badlands, canyons, and ravines shaped by an ancient lake. Notable landmarks include the 11th-century Arab fortress, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of the Seven Towers&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031020332/geoparque-de-granada-circular-desde-galera-por-orce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce&lt;/a&gt; route specifically takes you through some of these geological formations. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5899483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galera&lt;/a&gt; village is also known for its cave houses.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Orce?
A: While many routes in Orce are moderate to difficult, there are options for less experienced riders. The region offers some easier paths suitable for families, though specific dedicated 'family-friendly' routes are fewer. It's advisable to check the difficulty grade of individual routes on Komoot; there are 5 routes classified as 'easy' out of 60 available mountain bike tours in the area. These typically feature less elevation and smoother terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Orce?
A: The Orce region, being an outdoor destination, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, specific regulations regarding leashes or access might vary depending on the exact trail or protected areas within the Granada Geopark. It's always recommended to keep your dog under control, especially when encountering other trail users or wildlife, and to check local signage for any specific rules.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Orce?
A: Orce, located in southeastern Spain, experiences hot summers and mild winters. The best time for mountain biking is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, temperatures are more moderate, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, especially in the arid badlands, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Orce?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Orce are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209016067/orce-al-bosque-circular-por-el-cordel-de-puerto-de-chirivel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031020332/geoparque-de-granada-circular-desde-galera-por-orce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes are convenient for exploring the diverse landscapes without needing shuttle services.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Orce?
A: The mountain biking experience in Orce is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the rugged mountain scenery, the unique geological formations of the Granada Geopark, and the challenging nature of many routes. The diverse terrain, transitioning from valleys to arid badlands, also contributes to a memorable and varied ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in the Orce area?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes, especially those starting from Orce or nearby villages like Galera or Huéscar, will have designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of the settlements. For specific trailheads, it's best to check the route details on Komoot, as users often provide information on parking spots. Generally, you can find parking in the towns before heading out onto the trails.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Orce region?
A: Mountain biking in the general Orce area typically does not require specific permits for most trails. However, as parts of the region fall within the Granada Geopark and potentially other natural protected areas, it's always advisable to be aware of local regulations. Always stick to marked trails and respect any signage regarding access restrictions, especially in sensitive ecological zones.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Orce?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails available around Orce on Komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes, offering a wide range of options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: This citadel, also known as Orce castle, is an old fortress dating from the Nasrid period. Thanks to the various reforms that have been carried out, it still preserves the seven original towers. It was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985.
Nice section of trail that passes a helipad and a few other structures as it passes through a forest.
